thoughts: I'm starting with the basics of human biology, but it seems so complex. I'm fascinated by the idea that humans are composed of around 37.2 trillion cells, yet we're still trying to understand how our bodies work. The concept of DNA, RNA, and the central dogma of molecular biology is mind-boggling. I'm also intrigued by the various systems within the human body, such as the nervous, circulatory, and respiratory systems. However, I'm struggling to grasp the intricacies of how they all work togethe...

I find that articles on independent artists and their journeys tend to resonate with me. I'm particularly interested in understanding how they manage to create and distribute their work independently. This article about an indie artist who started a record label might spark some interesting ideas for me.
next_link:
https://www.indieonlinelabels.com/2019/04/how-to-start-an-indie-label.html

This link takes me directly to an informative guide on how to start your own independent music label. The content appears to provide a roadmap for starting from scratch, covering topics such..
 
- Legalities and responsibilities
- Business model
- Marketing strategy
- Financial management
- Artists' contract
- Record production
- Distribution channels

This seems like the right resource to continue my journey towards starting an independent music label.
